tool_args.label: Standard Follow-Up Report: ADA Compliance Report

payload.title: Standard Follow-Up Report: Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Compliance Report

evidence_refs.source_quote: Standard Follow-Up Report: Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Compliance Report
AMERICANS WITH DISABILITIES ACT COMPLIANCE REPORT December 2023
This report is the annual update on the status of compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) at MTA New York City Transit.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: This report is the annual update on the status of compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) at MTA New York City Transit.

evidence_refs.source_quote: This report is the annual update on the status of compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) at MTA New York City Transit.

payload.title: Senior Vice President, Stations Business Unit

evidence_refs.source_quote: William Montanile, P.E. Senior Vice President, Stations Business Unit

payload.description: In June 2022, Governor Kathy Hochul announced that the MTA and accessibility advocates reached a class action settlement.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In June 2022, Governor Kathy Hochul announced that the Metropolitan Transportation Authority and accessibility advocates reached a class action settlement

payload.title: Chair and CEO
payload.description: MTA, headed by Chair and CEO Janno Lieber

evidence_refs.source_quote: the MTA, headed by Chair and CEO Janno Lieber

payload.description: Established in February 2019 as successor to the CCC. All-volunteer group of 18 community members committed to working with NYCT on accessibility issues.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In February 2019, the Advisory Committee for Transit Accessibility (ACTA) was established as a successor to the CCC. ACTA is an all-volunteer group of 18 community members committed to working with NYCT on a range of accessibility issues.

payload.description: Established as a successor to the Transportation Disabled Committee, as a method for the disability community to provide their input in the selection of several Key stations.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The NYCT ADA Compliance Coordination Committee (CCC) was established as a successor to the Transportation Disabled Committee, as a method for the disability community to provide their input in the selection of several Key stations.

payload.description: In 2005 the Office of ADA Compliance partnered with the Department of Subways to administer the ADA Sensitivity Training Course.

evidence_refs.source_quote: in 2005 the Office of ADA Compliance partnered with the Department of Subways to administer the ADA Sensitivity Training Course.

tool_args.label: Mayor's Office for People with Disabilities (MOPD)

payload.entity_name: Mayor's Office for People with Disabilities (MOPD)
payload.description: Annual reports are sent to the Mayor's Office for People with Disabilities (MOPD) on the status of ADA compliance within NYCT.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Annual reports are sent to the Mayor's Office for People with Disabilities (MOPD) and the New York State Governor's Office on the status of ADA compliance within NYCT.

payload.description: NYCT's plan to make 100 stations accessible by 2020. 99 Key stations are accessible; the final station (68 Street, 6 line, Manhattan) is under construction for accessibility.

NYCT committed to an additional 50 accessible stations as part of a program called Fast Forward. Goal: ensure no subway rider was more than two stops away from an accessible station anywhere in the system. Commitment increased in 2019 from 50 stations to 70 stations.

evidence_refs.source_quote: in 2018, NYCT committed to an additional 50 accessible stations as part of a program called Fast Forward.

payload.description: The final Key station is in construction for accessibility. 68 Street on the 6 line in Manhattan. Projected Place-in-Service December 2024, Substantial Completion December 2024.
ACTA held its first meeting in June of 2019.

evidence_refs.source_quote: ACTA held its first meeting in June of 2019.

payload.description: Governor Kathy Hochul announced that the Metropolitan Transportation Authority and accessibility advocates reached a class action settlement that affirms the MTA's commitment towards accessibility in the subway system.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In June 2022, Governor Kathy Hochul announced that the Metropolitan Transportation Authority and accessibility advocates reached a class action settlement

payload.description: A public event was held on February 6, 2019, where every geographic area across the system was reviewed and feedback was received on priority stations.

evidence_refs.source_quote: A public event was held on February 6, 2019, where every geographic area across the system was reviewed and feedback was received on priority stations.
